mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals an imminent labor market deterioration, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days as investors price in lower corporate earnings.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and stable housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength without housing sector drag, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative forward returns in > 55% of cases over 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average reflects expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days specifically during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: Signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) when FEDFUNDS changes by > 0.25% in the same month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening labor demand, which correlates with positive short-term S&P 500 returns over five trading days as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistical correlation (p > 0.10) with 5-day forward returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.35% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust aggregate demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as corporate revenue forecasts are upgraded.
failure_condition: Divergence between GDPC1 and RSAFS trends nullifies the signal, resulting in flat or negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in the unemployment rate and decline in payroll employment signals a sharp labor market contraction, driving negative S&P 500 returns over five trading days specifically during recessionary transitions.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict negative returns when the economy is in a stable expansion phase (Rank IC < 0.01).
